subject: Math

lesson.story_mission: Join the Skill World lab to master solve one-step equations and inequalities and represent solutions.
lesson.objective: solve one-step equations and inequalities and represent solutions
lesson.concept_explanation: This mission teaches how to reason about solve one-step equations and inequalities and represent solutions with symbols, models, and steps.
lesson.common_misconception: A common mistake is treating symbols like decoration instead of using their math meaning.
lesson.real_world_connection: These ideas help describe prices, patterns, distances, and changing quantities.
lesson.lesson_summary: Read the structure first, use the model, then check that your answer makes the original statement true.

page_audio.text: Read This Page. This lesson teaches solve one-step equations and inequalities and represent solutions. Math symbols have jobs, so read them in order and connect them to the visual model. Explain why the answer works, not just what the answer is.
page_audio.label: Read This Page

worked_examples.text: For x + 4 = 11, subtract 4 from both sides to get x = 7.

page_audio.text: Read This Page. Watch for solve one-step equations and inequalities and represent solutions. Notice the important math language and connect each symbol to what it means. Use the model on the page to explain your thinking before choosing or typing an answer.
page_audio.label: Read This Page

worked_examples.text: x greater than 3 is shown with an open circle at 3 and an arrow to the right.

page_audio.text: Read This Page. Demo for solve one-step equations and inequalities and represent solutions. Notice the important math language and connect each symbol to what it means. Use the model on the page to explain your thinking before choosing or typing an answer.
page_audio.label: Read This Page

hint_ladder: Read the symbols and units carefully.
Use the visual model to plan the first step.
Check your result in the original expression, equation, or relationship.
